metric_value: Aberdeen field experience: enterprises that build uncontrolled standalone data marts end up with contradictory business rules and enterprise-crippling data requests
notes: Primary empirical finding from Aberdeen client engagements

metric_value: Most enterprises have one or more inconsistent definitions for basic enterprise expression 'revenue' across systems
notes: Aberdeen field finding; data quality problem at the heart of warehouse need

metric_value: Begin with one data mart addressing a single subject area that links into an RDBMS-driven enterprise whole
notes: Factor 1: Aberdeen's integrated data mart architectural principle

metric_value: Enterprise must work from high-level model and common business metrics before building individual data marts
notes: Factor 2: prerequisite for integration; prevents contradictory definitions

metric_value: Use iterative method of building a road map for the enterprise to follow; each data mart funds the next
notes: Factor 3: ROI self-funding model for data mart expansion

metric_value: Involve end users throughout design; data marts must reflect business drivers embedded in end-user behavior
notes: Factor 4: business-driven design vs. IT-driven design

metric_value: Managing synchronization across data marts is critical; requires disciplined ETL and common data stewardship
notes: Factor 5: technical synchronization challenge for distributed data marts

metric_value: Use Rapid Application Deployment to maintain business momentum; demonstrate ROI early to secure continued funding
notes: Factor 6: delivery speed as competitive requirement

metric_value: NCR, Tandem, and Prism Solutions positioned as providers of industry-specific data warehouse templates and short-term consulting engagements
notes: Aberdeen identifies these suppliers as jump-start partners for integrated data mart initiatives

metric_name: RDBMS vs multidimensional DB strategic choice
metric_value: Aberdeen advocates RDBMS-based architecture over proprietary multidimensional databases for enterprise data marts; RDBMS provides flexibility and integration path
notes: Clear architectural recommendation against MDDB silos

metric_value: Standalone data marts: frequently commandeered by business units; adequately answer short-term objectives but prove shortsighted; risk of contradictory business rules
notes: Aberdeen classifies standalone data marts as problematic pattern

metric_name: Enterprise-wide data warehouse: top-down failure mode
metric_value: Early attempts at monolithic enterprise-wide warehouses failed due to rapidly changing business dynamics toppling top-down models
notes: Historical context: why big-bang warehouse projects failed; validates iterative approach

metric_name: RDBMS suitability for enterprise data warehouse
metric_value: RDBMS-driven technology is the preferred foundation; parallel-scalable hardware and RDBMS combine to create enterprise-capable warehouse platform
notes: Core technology stack recommendation

metric_value: Enterprise policy of encouraged fragmentation could be disastrous for the business; competitive disadvantage from incompatible data marts
notes: Aberdeen's strongest warning; prescient about 'data swamp' problems of 2010s

metric_value: Enterprises that followed integrated model begin generating ROI that underwrites subsequent data mart efforts; project-by-project experience creates compounding value
notes: Aberdeen's empirical observation from client engagements

metric_value: IS executives must build data access systems that reflect business drivers embedded in end-user behavior; IT-driven warehouses miss this requirement
notes: Aberdeen's core thesis on user-centric data architecture

metric_name: Integrated data marts as dominant BI architecture
metric_value: Aberdeen predicts integrated data mart approach (subject-by-subject building toward enterprise warehouse) will prove superior to standalone or top-down approaches
notes: Pairs with OBS-018

metric_value: Hub-and-spoke data warehouse architecture (matching Aberdeen's integrated model) became the dominant enterprise BI pattern through 2000s; standalone data marts caused exactly the fragmentation Aberdeen warned about
notes: Prediction confirmed by industry evolution; Ralph Kimball's dimensional modeling (bottom-up) and Inmon's enterprise warehouse (top-down) both validated the integrated approach Aberdeen prescribed

metric_value: Aberdeen: enterprises moving away from integrated spirit will squander ROI and be ill-prepared for competitive battles
notes: Pairs with OBS-020

metric_value: 'Data swamp' problem became widespread by 2013-2015 as unmanaged data lakes replicated exactly the fragmentation Aberdeen warned about; data governance and data catalog tools emerged to address this
notes: Aberdeen's 1996 fragmentation warning proved exactly right in data lake era; problem recurred at larger scale

metric_value: Aberdeen identifies distinct data mart themes: sales/marketing, financial, manufacturing, supply chain — each requiring subject-specific modeling with common enterprise metrics
notes: Taxonomy of data mart subject areas from Aberdeen field experience

metric_value: 'Factory ready' data infrastructure requires: parallel-scalable hardware, RDBMS, ETL tools, and data quality/transformation capabilities before data mart build
notes: Aberdeen's infrastructure prerequisites checklist

metric_value: Data transformation specialists (Prism Solutions et al.) provide critical ETL capabilities; short-term consulting engagements with suppliers a viable jump-start strategy
notes: Aberdeen endorses consulting-led implementation as ROI accelerator

metric_name: Industry drivers for data architecture change
metric_value: Global trading, global risk assessment, and competitive intelligence needs cited as primary business drivers accelerating data mart adoption in 1996
notes: Business context for 1996 data architecture urgency

metric_value: Proprietary multidimensional database technologies optimize for domain-specific queries but create proprietary lock-in; RDBMS preferred for enterprise integration
notes: Aberdeen's architectural warning against MDDB-based data marts
